tool.name: skani triangle
tool.description: compute all-to-all ANI matrix for a set of genomes
tool.command: skani triangle -t \${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1} @PRESET_CMD@ @ALGORITHM_CMD@ --min-af $output_opts.min_af #if str($output_opts.both_min_af).strip() --both-min-af $output_opts.both_min_af #end if $output_opts.short_header $individual_seqs #if str($output_mode.mode) == "sparse" -E $output_mode.ci $output_mode.detailed $output_mode.diagonal #else #if str($output_mode.mode) == "full" --full-matrix #end if $output_mode.distance $output_mode.diagonal #end if #for $g in $genomes '$g' #end for -o '$output'

param.name: genomes
param.label: Genomes
param.help: Two or more FASTA files for all-to-all comparison (gzip accepted).

param.name: individual_seqs
param.label: Treat each sequence as its own genome
param.help: Use individual sequences in multi-fastas as separate genomes.

param.name: mode
param.label: Output format
param.help: Lower-triangular matrix is the standard square-matrix output (Phylip-like). Full matrix outputs a square symmetric matrix. Sparse edge-list outputs one row per pair, like 'skani dist'.

param.label: Output distance (100 - ANI)
param.help: Emit a distance matrix instead of an ANI similarity matrix.

param.label: Output distance (100 - ANI)
param.help: Emit a distance matrix instead of an ANI similarity matrix.

param.label: Include 90% confidence intervals
param.help: Output [5%, 95%] ANI confidence intervals using percentile bootstrap.

param.label: Detailed output
param.help: Print additional info including contig N50s and more.

param.label: Minimum aligned fraction (%)
param.help: Only output ANI values where one genome has aligned fraction greater than this value.

param.label: Minimum aligned fraction for BOTH genomes (%)
param.help: If set, only output ANI values where both genomes have aligned fraction greater than this value.

param.label: Short header names
param.help: Only display the first part of contig names (before the first whitespace).

tool.help:
**skani triangle**
Compute an all-to-all ANI/AF matrix for a set of genome assemblies. Useful for clustering MAGs at the strain or species level, or for building distance matrices for downstream phylogenetic analysis.
-----
**Inputs**
- Two or more FASTA files (``.fasta`` or ``.fasta.gz``). For very large input sets, consider sketching the genomes first with ``skani sketch`` (when available) and passing the sketches as input.
-----
**Output formats**
- **Lower-triangular matrix** (default) -- a Phylip-like square matrix with the number of genomes on the first line, then one row per genome. Use *Output distance* to invert the values to ``100 - ANI``. - **Full matrix** -- a square symmetric matrix. - **Sparse edge list** -- one row per genome pair that passes ``--min-af``, in the same format as ``skani dist``. Use this for very large inputs where the dense matrix would be unwieldy.
-----
**Tips**
- For viral / plasmid inputs use the **Small genomes** preset. - For MAGs with low N50, use the **Slow** preset to improve aligned-fraction accuracy. - Pairs below the ``--min-af`` threshold are reported as ``0.00`` in the dense matrix outputs and omitted entirely from the sparse edge list.
